purpose: Registered rule for selecting the drift instrument on EXISTING pilot texts (selection data). The confirmatory gate runs later on fresh records under prereg-v2. Committed BEFORE any candidate instrument is scored on the texts.

candidates.desc: Bidirectional NLI: drift = 1 - mean(P_entail(a->b), P_entail(b->a)) using a DeBERTa-v3 multi-dataset MNLI-class checkpoint
1 - Jaccard overlap of content words (stopword-stripped, lowercased)
Existing BERTScore-F1 drift (comparator only; may win only by the same rule as others)

evaluation_data: existing pilot-v2 targeted and control rationale texts; PAIRS RESTRICTED to calibration-fold candidates (even identity-sorted positions) — verification-fold texts stay untouched
selection_rule: winner = candidate with the highest unpaired AUROC(targeted vs control drift) on the calibration-fold pairs, REQUIRING paired median delta > 0 on candidates having both drifts; ties within 0.01 AUROC break toward lower CC1 risk (jaccard > embed > nli > bertscore reversed-risk order), then toward cheaper compute
notes: AlignScore was named by the panel but is excluded from this round unless its package installs cleanly on CPU without pinning conflicts; if included later, it joins under the same rule BEFORE scoring (record an amendment).
